docs.title: dot-man
docs.text:
Dotfile manager with git-powered branching

- :material-download: **Install** — `pip install dotman-git` - :material-rocket-launch: **Quick Start** — Get running in 60 seconds - :material-console: **Commands** — Full CLI reference - :material-book-open-variant: **Concepts** — How dot-man works

docs.title: What is dot-man?
docs.text:
dot-man stores your dotfiles in a git repository and uses branches as configuration profiles. Each branch is a complete, deployable snapshot of your environment.

Your Machine                  dot-man Repo               Any Machine
──────────────                ────────────               ───────────
~/.bashrc      ──── save ──►  branch: main  ── deploy ──►  ~/.bashrc
~/.config/nvim ──── save ──►  branch: work  ── deploy ──►  ~/.config/nvim
~/.gitconfig   ──── save ──►  branch: server               ~/.gitconfig

Secrets are never committed. API keys, tokens and passwords are automatically detected, encrypted locally in a vault, and replaced with hashes in the repository before any commit.

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text:
pip install dotman-git
dot-man init
dot-man save "my first config"

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text: Feature Description Branch Profiles Switch between work, personal, server configs with one command Secret Vault API keys encrypted locally, never committed to git Automatic Discovery Detects 30+ common dotfile locations Shell Hooks Pre/post deploy scripts, quickshell integration Tag Snapshots Tag and rollback to any point in time Template Variables Machine-specific configs with {{ MACHINE }}, {{ OS }} Conditional Config {{ if OS == "darwin" }} blocks for cross-platform configs Package Bootstrap Install packages via brew/apt/dnf/pacman on new machines JSON Output --json flag on status for CI/scripting integration

docs.title: Commands Overview
docs.text: dot-man provides the following commands:

docs.title: Core Workflow
docs.text: Command Description init Initialize dot-man repository navigate Switch between branch profiles save Save current dotfiles to branch status Show current state and changes deploy Deploy branch files to system

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Command Description config View/edit configuration bootstrap Install system packages discover Auto-discover dotfile locations

docs.title: Inspection
docs.text: Command Description audit Scan files for secrets diff Show uncommitted changes log Show commit history doctor Check system health verify Verify repo integrity

docs.title: Tags & Remote
docs.text: Command Description tag Create/list/delete tag snapshots remote Configure remote sync sync Push/pull to remote

docs.title: Encryption & Vault
docs.text: Command Description vault Manage encrypted secrets encrypt GPG/AGE file encryption

docs.title: Hooks & Templates
docs.text: Command Description hooks Manage deploy hooks template Show template variable values

docs.title: Shell & Misc
docs.text: Command Description completions Install shell completions migrate Migrate from other dotfile managers watch Watch for file changes interface Manage CLI interface mode

docs.title: audit
docs.text: Scan tracked files for secrets, API keys, tokens, and passwords.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: dot-man audit

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text: Scans all files currently tracked by dot-man for: - API keys (AWS, GitHub, Google, etc.) - Private keys (RSA, SSH, PGP) - Passwords in config files - Connection strings - JWT tokens

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --fix Automatically replace detected secrets with vault tokens --json Output results as JSON --section <name> Audit only a specific section

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Scan all tracked files
dot-man audit

# Auto-fix detected secrets
dot-man audit --fix

# Audit one section
dot-man audit --section main

docs.title: bootstrap
docs.text: Install system packages on a new machine using your system's package manager.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: dot-man bootstrap

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Detects your system's package manager (brew, apt, dnf, pacman, zypper, nix, xbps)
  2. Reads packages from config [bootstrap] section
  3. Installs missing packages

docs.title: Detection Order
docs.text: Manager Platform brew macOS (Homebrew) apt Debian/Ubuntu dnf Fedora pacman Arch/Manjaro zypper openSUSE nix NixOS xbps Void Linux

docs.title: Config
docs.text:
[bootstrap]
packages = ["git", "vim", "neovim", "ripgrep", "fzf"]

# Per-manager overrides
packages_brew = ["htop", "node"]
packages_apt = ["htop", "nodejs"]
packages_pacman = ["htop", "nodejs"]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --dry-run Show what would be installed --manager <name> Force a specific package manager

docs.title: config
docs.text: View and edit the dot-man configuration file.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
dot-man config
dot-man config get <key>
dot-man config set <key> <value>

docs.title: config (no args)
docs.text: Opens the config file in your default editor ($EDITOR).

docs.title: config get <key>
docs.text:
Get a config value:

dot-man config get settings.default_branch
dot-man config get main.paths

docs.title: config set <key> <value>
docs.text:
Set a config value:

dot-man config set settings.default_branch main
dot-man config set main.deploy_method symlink

docs.title: config tutorial
docs.text:
Interactive tutorial for setting up your first configuration. Walks through creating sections, adding paths, and configuring hooks step by step.

dot-man config tutorial

docs.title: doctor
docs.text: Check system health and dot-man configuration.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: dot-man doctor

docs.title: Checks
docs.text:
• Git installation and version
  • Repository structure integrity
  • Config file validity
  • Vault accessibility
  • Shell completions installed
  • Required Python packages

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --json Output results as JSON --fix Attempt to auto-fix detected issues

docs.title: navigate
docs.text: Switch between branch profiles. This is the primary command for managing different configurations.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
dot-man navigate <branch>
dot-man nav <branch>          # alias

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
When you navigate to a branch, dot-man:

  1. Auto-saves current changes (unless --no-save)
  2. Switches the git branch
  3. Deploys files from the new branch to your system
  4. Runs hooks (on_activate, on_deactivate)
  5. Updates the config to track the new active branch

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --no-save Skip auto-save before switching --no-deploy Switch branch without deploying files --no-hooks Skip running hook scripts --dry-run Preview changes without applying

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Switch to work profile
dot-man navigate work

# Preview what would change
dot-man navigate personal --dry-run

# Switch without saving current changes
dot-man navigate server --no-save

docs.title: Branch Diff Preview
docs.text:
With --dry-run, navigate shows a preview of:

  • Files that would be saved from current branch
  • Files that would be deployed from target branch
  • Hooks that would run
  • Summary of changes by section

docs.title: diff
docs.text:
Show uncommitted changes in tracked files.

dot-man diff
dot-man diff --branch work    # Compare with another branch

docs.title: log
docs.text:
Show commit history for the current branch.

dot-man log
dot-man log --limit 10
dot-man log --oneline

docs.title: tag
docs.text:
Create and manage tag snapshots for rollback.

dot-man tag create v1.0         # Tag current state
dot-man tag list                # List all tags
dot-man tag delete v1.0         # Delete a tag
dot-man navigate main --tag v1.0  # Deploy a tag snapshot

docs.title: remote
docs.text:
Configure remote sync.

dot-man remote add origin https://github.com/user/dotfiles.git
dot-man remote list

docs.title: sync
docs.text:
Push/pull to remote.

dot-man sync push
dot-man sync pull

docs.title: discover
docs.text:
Auto-discover common dotfile locations.

dot-man discover
dot-man discover --preview     # Show without adding

docs.title: hooks
docs.text:
Manage deploy hooks.

dot-man hooks list
dot-man hooks run pre-deploy

docs.title: template
docs.text:
Show template variable values.

dot-man template              # All variables
dot-man template MACHINE     # Specific variable

docs.title: completions
docs.text:
Install shell completions.

dot-man completions install
dot-man completions status

docs.title: watch
docs.text:
Watch for file changes and auto-save.

dot-man watch
dot-man watch --interval 30

docs.title: interface
docs.text:
Manage CLI interface mode (standard vs enhanced).

dot-man interface
dot-man interface standard

docs.title: migrate
docs.text:
Migrate from other dotfile managers.

dot-man migrate chezmoi
dot-man migrate yadm
dot-man migrate stow

docs.title: save
docs.text: Save current dotfile changes to the active branch.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: dot-man save [MESSAGE]

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
1. Scans all configured paths for changes
  2. Detects and strips secrets (replaced with vault tokens)
  3. Commits changes with the provided message
  4. Reports files saved, secrets detected, hooks run

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --force Save even if no tracked files changed --dry-run Preview what would be saved --section <name> Only save specific section

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Save with a message
dot-man save "updated neovim config"

# Preview changes
dot-man save --dry-run

# Only save one section
dot-man save --section main "bashrc updates"

# Force save even with no tracked changes
dot-man save --force "vault rotation"

docs.title: status
docs.text: Show current state: active branch, modified files, and summary.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: dot-man status

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --json Output structured JSON for CI/scripting --verbose Show all files, not just changed ones

docs.title: Text Output
docs.text:
Branch: work
Remote: https://github.com/user/dotfiles.git
Repository: ~/.config/dot-man/repo

Sections:
  main (3 files)
    M ~/.bashrc
    M ~/.gitconfig
    M ~/.config/nvim/init.vim

  work (1 file)
    M ~/.config/slack/settings.json

Summary:
  Tracked: 12
  Modified: 4
  Untracked: 2
  Secrets detected: 1

docs.title: JSON Output
docs.text:
dot-man status --json

{
  "branch": "work",
  "remote": "https://github.com/user/dotfiles.git",
  "repository": "~/.config/dot-man/repo",
  "sections": [
    {
      "name": "main",
      "files": [
        {"path": "~/.bashrc", "status": "modified"},
        {"path": "~/.gitconfig", "status": "modified"}
      ]
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "tracked": 12,
    "modified": 4,
    "untracked": 2
  }
}

docs.title: vault
docs.text: Manage the encrypted secret vault.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
dot-man vault status
dot-man vault rotate-key

docs.title: vault status
docs.text:
Show vault status: number of secrets, key age, vault file location.

dot-man vault status

docs.title: vault rotate-key
docs.text:
Rotate the Fernet encryption key. Old key is backed up to .key.bak.

dot-man vault rotate-key

This re-encrypts all secrets with the new key.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Secrets are stored in ~/.config/dot-man/vault.json
  • Encrypted with Fernet (AES-128-CBC + HMAC)
  • Key stored at ~/.config/dot-man/.key
  • Old key backed up during rotation at .key.bak

docs.title: Branch Profiles
docs.text: Branches in dot-man are configuration profiles. Each branch contains a complete snapshot of your dotfiles for a specific context (work, personal, server, etc.).

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
~/.config/dot-man/repo/
├── main          ← personal config
├── work          ← work config
├── server        ← server config
└── minimal       ← minimal setup

When you dot-man navigate work, dot-man:

  1. Saves current changes on the active branch
  2. Switches to the work branch
  3. Deploys all files from work to your home directory
  4. Runs activation hooks

docs.title: Creating Profiles
docs.text:
# Start from current state
dot-man init
dot-man save "personal config"
dot-man branch work
dot-man navigate work
# ... customize for work ...
dot-man save "work config"

docs.title: Branch vs Tag
docs.text: Branch Tag Mutable Yes — changes over time No — point-in-time snapshot Use for Active profiles Rollback points Example work, personal v1.0, backup-2024-01-01

docs.title: Conditional Config
docs.text: dot-man supports conditional blocks in tracked files for cross-platform configs.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
{{ if VAR == "value" }}
  content when condition is true
{{ endif }}

docs.title: Supported Operators
docs.text:
• == — equals
  • != — not equals

docs.title: OS-specific
docs.text:
# ~/.bashrc
{{ if OS == "darwin" }}
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH"
alias ls="ls -G"
{{ endif }}

{{ if OS == "linux" }}
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
alias ls="ls --color=auto"
{{ endif }}

docs.title: Machine-specific
docs.text:
# ~/.gitconfig
{{ if MACHINE == "work-laptop" }}
[url "git@github.com:company/"]
    insteadOf = https://github.com/company/
{{ endif }}

docs.title: Negation
docs.text:
{{ if USER != "root" }}
# Only for non-root users
export EDITOR=nvim
{{ endif }}

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Templates are processed during deploy and save
  2. Conditionals are evaluated first
  3. Simple {{VAR}} substitution happens after
  4. If a variable is undefined, the conditional block is skipped

docs.title: Deploy Methods
docs.text: dot-man supports two methods for deploying files from the repository to your system.

docs.title: Copy (Default)
docs.text:
Files are physically copied from the repo to your home directory.

[main]
deploy_method = "copy"

Pros: - Files are independent — editing deployed files doesn't affect the repo - Works with any application (no broken symlink issues) - Atomic writes (POSIX os.replace) prevent corruption

Cons: - Changes to deployed files are not reflected in the repo - Must save to capture changes

docs.title: Symlink
docs.text:
Files are symlinked from the repo to your home directory.

[main]
deploy_method = "symlink"

Pros: - Changes to files are immediately reflected in the repo - No need to save after editing

Cons: - Breaking the symlink breaks the file - Some apps don't work well with symlinks - Must keep the repo path stable

docs.title: Per-Section
docs.text:
Each section can use a different method:

[main]
deploy_method = "copy"

[symlinked]
deploy_method = "symlink"
paths = ["~/.config/nvim/", "~/.config/tmux/"]

docs.title: Comparison
docs.text: Copy Symlink Default Yes No File independence Yes No Auto-sync No Yes Breakability Low Higher Recommended for Most configs Quick iteration

docs.title: Hooks
docs.text: Hooks are shell commands that run at specific points during save and deploy.

docs.title: Hook Types
docs.text: Hook When It Runs pre_deploy Before files are copied/symlinked post_deploy After files are copied/symlinked on_activate When branch is switched to on_deactivate When branch is switched from

docs.title: Config
docs.text:
[main]
on_activate = "source ~/.bashrc"
on_deactivate = "echo 'Leaving main config'"

[work]
pre_deploy = "systemctl --user stop neovim"
post_deploy = "systemctl --user start neovim"
on_activate = "source ~/.config/work/env.sh"

docs.title: Built-in Aliases
docs.text: Alias Description quickshell_reload Reload quickshell config quickshell_restart Restart quickshell quickshell_validate Validate quickshell config

docs.title: Example: Quickshell Integration
docs.text:
[quickshell]
paths = ["~/.config/quickshell/"]
post_deploy = "quickshell_reload"
on_deactivate = "killall qs 2>/dev/null || true"
on_activate = "qs -c ii &"

docs.title: Shell Variable
docs.text:
Within hooks, the $DOT_MAN_BRANCH variable contains the active branch name.

on_activate = "echo \"Switched to $DOT_MAN_BRANCH config\""

docs.title: Secret Detection
docs.text: dot-man automatically detects and removes secrets before any commit.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Detection: Scans files for patterns (API keys, tokens, passwords)
  2. Extraction: Removes the secret value from the file
  3. Tokenization: Replaces with VAULT_TOKEN_<hash>
  4. Encryption: Encrypts the real value in the vault using Fernet
# Before commit:
GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_abc123xyz

# After save:
GITHUB_TOKEN=VAULT_TOKEN_7f3a8b2c

docs.title: Supported Patterns
docs.text:
• AWS Access Key / Secret Key
  • GitHub tokens (ghp_, gho_, github_pat_)
  • Google API keys
  • Private keys (RSA, SSH, PGP)
  • Generic API keys, tokens, passwords
  • Connection strings
  • JWT tokens

docs.title: Custom Patterns
docs.text:
Add custom patterns in dot-man.toml:

[settings]
custom_secret_patterns = [
    "MY_SECRET_[A-Z0-9]+",
    "internal_key_[a-f0-9]{32}",
]

docs.title: Vault
docs.text: Secrets are stored in ~/.config/dot-man/vault.json: - Encrypted with Fernet (AES-128-CBC + HMAC) - Key at ~/.config/dot-man/.key - Never committed to git

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
dot-man audit          # Scan for secrets
dot-man audit --fix    # Auto-replace with tokens
dot-man vault status   # Check vault
dot-man vault rotate-key  # Rotate encryption key

docs.title: Template Variables
docs.text: Template variables let you customize dotfiles per machine, OS, or user.

docs.title: Built-in Variables
docs.text: Variable Example Value {{ MACHINE }} work-laptop {{ USER }} john {{ OS }} linux, darwin, windows {{ ARCH }} x86_64, aarch64 {{ HOSTNAME }} john-laptop

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
In any tracked file:

# ~/.bashrc
if [ "{{ OS }}" = "darwin" ]; then
    alias ls="ls -G"
else
    alias ls="ls --color=auto"
fi

# ~/.gitconfig
[user]
    name = {{ USER }}

docs.title: Conditional Blocks
docs.text:
# macOS-specific
{{ if OS == "darwin" }}
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH"
{{ endif }}

# Linux-specific
{{ if OS == "linux" }}
export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"
{{ endif }}

docs.title: Custom Variables
docs.text:
Define custom variables in config:

[settings]
template_vars = { EDITOR = "nvim", SHELL = "zsh" }

docs.title: Disable Templates
docs.text:
Per section:

[main]
render_templates = false

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: dot-man is a Python CLI tool built with Click.

docs.title: Module Structure
docs.text:
dot_man/
├── cli/                    # CLI commands
│   ├── interface.py       # CLI group definition
│   ├── main.py            # Entry point
│   ├── common.py          # Shared utilities, completions
│   ├── completions.py     # Shell completion logic
│   ├── navigate_cmd.py    # navigate command
│   ├── navigate_preview.py # Diff/preview display
│   ├── config_cmd.py      # config command
│   ├── config_tutorial.py # Interactive tutorial
│   ├── status_cmd.py      # status command
│   ├── save_cmd.py        # save command
│   ├── bootstrap_cmd.py   # bootstrap command
│   ├── hooks_cmd.py       # hooks command
│   └── vault_cmd.py       # vault command
├── core.py                # GitManager (branch, commit, diff)
├── global_config.py       # Config parsing, template substitution
├── operations.py          # Business logic (save, deploy, status)
├── section.py             # Section class (config sections)
├── files.py               # File operations (copy, symlink, deploy)
├── secrets.py             # Secret detection patterns
├── vault.py               # Fernet encryption vault
├── branch_ops.py          # Branch operations (diff, merge)
├── tag_ops.py             # Tag management
├── save_deploy_ops.py     # Save/deploy operations
└── remote_ops.py          # Remote sync operations

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
User runs: dot-man navigate work
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│  navigate_cmd   │ ← CLI parsing, flags
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         ▼
┌─────────────────┐
│   operations    │ ← Business logic
└────────┬────────┘
    ┌────┴────┐
    ▼         ▼
┌────────┐ ┌────────┐
│ files  │ │  core  │
└────────┘ └────────┘
  copy/     git ops
  symlink   commit
            branch

docs.title: Key Design Decisions
docs.text:
1. Copy over symlink — Default deploy method uses physical copies. Prevents broken symlinks when branch structure changes.

  2. Secrets never enter git — Fernet encryption with local vault. API keys replaced with vault tokens before commit.

  3. POSIX atomic writes — os.replace() prevents dotfile corruption during system crashes.

  4. Resilient hooks — Hook failures are collected and reported, never abort the main operation.

  5. No external dependencies — Only stdlib + click + cryptography. No Jinja2 for templates (custom regex parser).

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
# Clone
git clone https://github.com/BeshoyEhab/dot-man.git
cd dot-man

# Install with dev dependencies
uv sync --extra dev

# Run checks
uv run black dot_man/ tests/
uv run ruff check dot_man/ tests/
uv run mypy dot_man/ --ignore-missing-imports
uv run pytest tests/ -v

docs.title: Pre-commit Hooks
docs.text:
pre-commit install

Runs Black, ruff, and mypy automatically on every commit.

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Formatter: Black (line length 88)
  • Linter: ruff (rules: E, F, W, I)
  • Type checker: mypy (--ignore-missing-imports)

docs.title: Adding Commands
docs.text:
1. Create dot_man/cli/<command>_cmd.py
  2. Import in dot_man/cli/__init__.py
  3. Add completions in dot_man/cli/common.py
  4. Add tests in tests/test_<command>_cmd.py
  5. Update docs and CHANGELOG

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
# All tests
uv run pytest tests/ -v

# Specific file
uv run pytest tests/test_core.py -v

# With coverage
uv run pytest --cov=dot_man --cov-report=term

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
<type>(<scope>): <subject>

feat(diff): add dot-man diff command
fix(vault): handle missing key file
docs(readme): update installation instructions
test(status): add JSON output tests

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: dot-man uses a TOML config file at ~/.config/dot-man/dot-man.toml.

docs.title: Basic Structure
docs.text:
# Global settings
[settings]
default_branch = "main"
deploy_method = "copy"

# Branch sections
[main]
paths = [
    "~/.bashrc",
    "~/.gitconfig",
    "~/.config/nvim/",
]
exclude = ["*.swp"]

[work]
paths = [
    "~/.bashrc",
    "~/.gitconfig",
    "~/.config/nvim/",
    "~/.config/slack/",
]

docs.title: Section Options
docs.text: Option Type Default Description paths list required Files/directories to track exclude list [] Glob patterns to exclude deploy_method string "copy" "copy" or "symlink" repo_path string auto Explicit repo path for single files secrets_filter bool true Enable secret detection render_templates bool true Enable template variable substitution pre_deploy string none Shell command before deploy post_deploy string none Shell command after deploy on_activate string none Shell command when branch activated on_deactivate string none Shell command when branch deactivated

docs.title: YAML Config
docs.text:
dot-man also supports YAML config files at ~/.config/dot-man/dot-man.yaml:

settings:
  default_branch: main

main:
  paths:
    - ~/.bashrc
    - ~/.gitconfig

docs.title: Bootstrap Packages
docs.text:
[bootstrap]
packages = ["git", "vim", "neovim", "ripgrep", "fzf"]

# Per-package manager overrides
packages_brew = ["htop", "node"]
packages_apt = ["htop", "nodejs"]

docs.title: From PyPI (Recommended)
docs.text: pip install dotman-git

docs.title: With pipx (Isolated)
docs.text: pipx install dotman-git

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/BeshoyEhab/dot-man.git
cd dot-man
pip install -e .

docs.title: Shell Completions
docs.text:
Shell completions are installed automatically on first run. To install manually:

dot-man completions install

Supports bash, zsh, and fish.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • Git

docs.title: 1. Initialize
docs.text:
dot-man init

This creates: - ~/.config/dot-man/ — dot-man home directory - ~/.config/dot-man/dot-man.toml — config file - ~/.config/dot-man/repo/ — bare git repository

docs.title: 2. Discover Dotfiles
docs.text:
dot-man auto-discovers common dotfile locations:

dot-man discover

This shows detected files and adds them to your config.

docs.title: 3. Save
docs.text:
Save your current dotfiles to a branch:

dot-man save "initial config"

This: 1. Scans configured paths for changes 2. Strips secrets and encrypts them in the vault 3. Commits everything to the current branch

docs.title: 4. Switch
docs.text:
Switch to a different configuration profile:

dot-man navigate work

This saves current changes, switches branch, and deploys files.

docs.title: 5. Deploy
docs.text:
Deploy a branch to a new machine:

dot-man deploy main

docs.title: Common Workflow
docs.text:
# Daily: save changes
dot-man save "updated vim config"

# Switch between profiles
dot-man navigate work
dot-man navigate personal

# Check what's different
dot-man status
dot-man diff
